title: Private companies Pull Out of South Australia Government芒鈧劉s $5 Billion Northern Water Project
content: The state government says its proposal for a more than $5 billion desalination plant and water pipeline across regional SA is not affected by the withdrawal of two energy companies once mooted as future customers.With a final investment decision due in the first half of next year, the ABC has confirmed Fortescue and Origin Energy were no longer involved in the Northern Water Supply project.The project, which is still undergoing a pre-feasibility study, involves building a desalination plant along the Spencer Gulf and connecting it to a new water pipeline stretching 600km across the state.
author: Analysts Account
